analysis_result: Median 1898 unique genes per nucleus at >=500 gene threshold (n=50,877 barcodes) vs. paper's reported median of 2249 unique genes per nucleus (n=43,365 nuclei)
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Raw 10X Genomics snRNA-seq matrices aggregated across 6 PNSTs
   - 50,877 barcodes passed model's >=500 gene threshold

B. COHORT DEFINITION (model vs. paper)
   Model: RAW 10X matrices; >=500 genes/barcode; no mito filter; no doublet removal -> 50,877 barcodes
   Paper: CellRanger aligned -> retained high-quality cells with >1500 unique genes AND <1.5% mitochondrial reads; genes in <3 cells removed -> 43,365 nuclei

C. VARIABLE CODING
   Unique genes = count of genes with nonzero UMI per barcode (both model and paper).

D. COMPUTATION
   Model median: 1898 [IQR 980-3246], mean 2290, n=50,877 (at >=500 threshold)
   Paper reported median: 2249 unique genes per nucleus across 43,365 nuclei

E. RESULT
   Relative difference: |2249 - 1898| / 2249 = 15.6% -> exceeds +/-10% concordance threshold. DISCREPANT.

F. ROOT CAUSE
   The paper's Methods (Droplet-based snRNA-seq section) explicitly state:
   'High-quality cells with >1500 unique genes detected and <1.5% reads attributed to mitochondrial transcripts were retained. Genes detected in less than three cells were removed.'
   The model used >=500 gene threshold (~3x less stringent) and omitted the mitochondrial filter. The model's more lenient threshold retains many low-quality barcodes (IQR lower bound 980) which drag down the median. Applying the paper's >1500 threshold plus mito filter on the same raw data would exclude the lower tail of barcodes, shifting the median up toward 2249. This is a classic COHORT_FILTER_DIFFERENCE: same underlying statistic (median unique genes/nucleus) computed on different QC-defined denominators.

G. ADDITIONAL NOTES
   Paper's n=43,365 nuclei vs. model's n=50,877 barcodes (+17% more barcodes retained) is consistent with the more permissive threshold used by the model. Doublet removal (scrublet) was not reported in paper methods but typical 10X Cell Ranger filtered matrix would exclude empty droplets -- model used RAW matrices which include empties, but the >=500 threshold removes most of these.
proposed_fix: Reproduce with the paper's exact QC: (1) use CellRanger-filtered matrices (or apply >1500 unique genes threshold, not >=500), (2) apply <1.5% mitochondrial fraction filter, (3) remove genes present in <3 cells. Recompute median unique genes/nucleus on the resulting ~43,365-nucleus cohort. Expected value should converge to ~2249.
